posts.title: No agent of the gov't may say to us, "I may have a gun, and you may not." To say that is to say "I am a nobleman, and you are a peasant. I am a master, and you are a slave." AMERICANS ARE NOT YOUR PEASANTS. WE ARE ALL NOBLEMEN. That is the most basic principle of what it means to be an American!

twitter.content:
Okay, time to explain guns to our new friends. 

Every day, when I leave the house, I attach a holstered handgun to my belt, under my shirt or coat. 

I would no more leave the house without a gun than I would walk around outdoors without shoes. 

Is it because I "need" a gun?
